claim: Context engineering for agents follows three core principles: offload (move context to filesystem), reduce (compress context via compaction/summarization/filtering), and isolate (use subagents for task isolation).
actionable: Apply the offload-reduce-isolate framework to your agent design; offload context to files, reduce message size via summarization, isolate complex subtasks in subagents.

claim: Persistent file systems (cloud.md, agent.md, memories directory) enable agents to maintain state across invocations and recover context without reloading the full conversation history.
actionable: Implement persistent markdown files (cloud.md at project level) where your agent can write and retrieve plans, memory, and state across multiple runs.

claim: Keep function-calling layers minimal (3-10 core tools) and offload detailed actions to scripts in a filesystem; this reduces tool confusion and system-prompt bloat vs. exposing hundreds of tools.
actionable: Design your agent with only atomic tools (bash, file manipulation, subtask creation); push detailed workflows into scripts that the agent discovers and executes dynamically.

claim: Progressive disclosure of actions (via skills folders with brief headers loaded upfront, full details read on demand) reduces initial context window usage while allowing agent access to complex workflows.
actionable: Organize agent skills in a directory structure where each skill has a brief header in the system prompt; full skill.md files are read only when the agent chooses to use them.

claim: Compaction (saving old tool results to files and referencing file paths) reduces context bloat without losing information; summarization (condensing message history) is irreversible and should be done carefully.
actionable: Use compaction first (offload old tool results to filesystem), apply summarization only after compaction and when hitting context limits (~95% of window).

claim: Subagent isolation with separate context windows for self-contained subtasks improves performance and allows sharing of parent agent context (message history and filesystem) when beneficial.
actionable: Break complex agent tasks into subagent workflows; pass necessary context selectively (message history or file references) rather than overwhelming subagents with full state.

claim: Task length is doubling approximately every seven months; production agents often exceed 100+ turns, making context management essential for cost and latency.
actionable: Plan for long-running agents; implement context engineering strategies (offload, reduce, isolate) from the start rather than retrofitting as context usage grows.
